goal: Run a complete computational ML research lab from literature to verified claims.

hypotheses.claim_candidate: Random train/test splits overstate generalization on sequential sensor data compared with blocked splits.
hypotheses.falsification_condition: Random and blocked split performance are similar within seed noise.
hypotheses.minimum_evidence_needed: Repeated-seed comparison where random split exceeds blocked split by more than seed noise.
hypotheses.why_it_matters: Sensor samples can be temporally or subject correlated; random splits can mix near-duplicates across train and test.

protocols.claim_blocked_if: split effects are within seed noise
protocols.claim_enabled_if: random_split mean exceeds blocked_split mean by more than seed noise
protocols.scientific_goal: Compare random train/test splitting against blocked sequential splitting on real sensor-style data.

question: Does feature scaling improve accuracy?

study_inference.rationale: `--study` was provided explicitly for a debug or ablation run.
